narrative.overview: Say No reads like a helpful little console that happens to dispense rejections. The entire interface is set in a single monospaced face, sits on a warm cream canvas, and relies on ASCII bracket glyphs instead of icons. The aesthetic is deadpan but warm: the joke lives in the generated words, not in decoration.
keyCharacteristics: 100% JetBrains Mono across every text role.
Warm cream canvas (#fdfcfc) in light mode; near-black (#201d1d) in dark mode.
Flat, shadowless surfaces at rest; depth via the dark output card and tonal layers.
ASCII bracket markers ([+], [-], [x], [ ], [v]) as the only iconography and state labels.
4px radius on interactive elements; 0px radius on containers and the output card.
A single saturated accent, reserved for errors: #ff3b30.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Only the danger color is saturated. Everything else stays inside the warm ink/canvas/ash family.

rules.name: The Mono-Everywhere Rule
rules.body: Every text role is rendered in JetBrains Mono.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: If an element is not hovered, focused, or pressed, it casts no shadow.

dos: Render every text role in JetBrains Mono.
Use 4px radius on buttons, selects, and filter chips.
Keep the output card as the single dark inversion on the page.
Use ASCII brackets for icons, bullets, and selection state.
Pair the warm cream canvas with near-black ink.
donts: Introduce a second font, display face, or italic style.
Use side-stripe borders greater than 1px as colored accents.
Add celebratory confetti, bounce, or elastic motion.
Default to generic SaaS cream/blue landing-page aesthetics.
Adopt a corporate / earnest self-help tone in visuals or copy.
Use shadows as decoration; shadows are state feedback only.
Put saturated accent colors on CTAs or marketing chrome.
